scope: B9.3 FROZEN event-marker map for the prospective randomized-audit acquisition. Fixed BEFORE any recording; the acquisition app must emit exactly these codes. Development-only, NO real data, NO tag.
principle: the acquisition app writes BOTH (a) integer trigger codes on the EEG trigger channel (redundant integrity) AND (b) a machine-readable companion trial log carrying the explicit trial_id (the canonical join key). Adherence is checked by trial_id join; the trigger cue code is an unambiguous cross-check of (C, Y_design).

cue_codes_C_x_Ydesign.note: tens digit = C+1, ones digit = Y_design+1; unambiguous 4-way encoding of the 2x2 cell. C=condition, Y_design=PRE-ASSIGNMENT cue class.

trial_id: 0..N-1 monotonic per the pinned assignment table; NOT encoded on the trigger channel (out of the 0-255 code range); carried explicitly in the companion trial log. Recoverable by joining the recording's per-trial sequence to the pinned table on (subject_slot, microblock, within-block order) as a redundant check.
integrity_rules: the emitted cue_code for a trial MUST equal cue_codes_C_x_Ydesign[(C,Y_design)] of that trial's pinned-table row
the companion trial log's (subject_slot, microblock, trial_id, C, Y_design) MUST equal the pinned table row for that trial_id (adherence)
timing jitter (t_cue_ms vs scheduled) is LOGGED as a logistics field; it does NOT change C/Y_design/trial_id
Y_design is the PRE-ASSIGNMENT cue -- it is NEVER overwritten by an observed response/outcome
not_authorized: encoding an observed/post-hoc label on the Y_design markers
regenerating the assignment at recording time
changing the code scheme after recording begins
